title: Verify the tipper recommissioning requirement after part replacement
summary: Reference procedure to confirm and communicate the documented maintenance requirement that a tipper must be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing tipper maintenance work, replacement activity, or return-to-service readiness to confirm whether the manual requires recommissioning after a part replacement.
not_for: Do not use as the actual tipper recommissioning procedure.
Do not use as a part replacement procedure.
Do not use to derive recommissioning steps not provided in this source section.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review maintenance status, verify the documented requirement, and communicate or record that recommissioning is required before return to service.

safety_notes: This source packet provides a maintenance requirement note only; it does not provide the recommissioning procedure in this section.
Do not return the tipper to service based only on this note without completing the applicable recommissioning procedure from the appropriate source section.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the maintenance manual note for section 7.3

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual
system_preconditions: A tipper maintenance or replacement condition is being reviewed

visual_references.description: Tipper component identification diagram that can help determine whether a tipper part was replaced.
Operator station HMI artifact from the tipper commissioning section, relevant as supporting evidence that commissioning content exists elsewhere in the manual.
Page 103 operator station screen artifact associated with nearby maintenance content in section 7.3.
Page 103 operator station screen artifact associated with nearby maintenance content in section 7.3.

steps.title: Identify whether a tipper part was replaced
steps.purpose: Determine whether the documented recommissioning requirement applies.
steps.instruction: Review the maintenance situation and identify whether any part on the tipper has been replaced.
steps.expected_result: A determination is made on whether a tipper part replacement occurred.
steps.healthy_condition: It is clear whether a tipper component was replaced.
steps.failure_condition: The maintenance status is unclear and it cannot be confirmed whether a part was replaced.

actions.label: Confirm part replacement status
actions.exact_action: Identify whether any part on the tipper has been replaced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Major tipper components that may have been replaced.

stop_or_escalate_if: It cannot be determined whether any tipper part was replaced.

source_evidence.text: Identify whether any part on the tipper has been replaced.
Figure 3-4 identifies major tipper components and supports part identification.

steps.title: Verify the documented recommissioning requirement
steps.purpose: Confirm the exact maintenance requirement from the source.
steps.instruction: Verify in the maintenance manual note that the tipper must be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.
steps.expected_result: The source requirement is confirmed from the manual.
steps.healthy_condition: The manual note is found and clearly states the recommissioning requirement.
steps.failure_condition: The requirement note cannot be located or cannot be confirmed from the supplied source evidence.

actions.label: Check maintenance note
actions.exact_action: Verify that the maintenance note requires the tipper to be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Nearby page 103 maintenance context in section 7.3.
Nearby page 103 maintenance context in section 7.3.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source note cannot be verified from the supplied packet.
Additional recommissioning steps are needed but are not present in this section.

source_evidence.text: NOTE: A tipper needs to be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.
Verify that the maintenance note requires the tipper to be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.

steps.title: Record or communicate the requirement before return to service
steps.purpose: Ensure the requirement is recognized before the tipper is returned to service.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate that re-commissioning is required before returning the tipper to service.
steps.expected_result: The recommissioning requirement is documented or communicated for the affected tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Relevant personnel understand that the tipper requires recommissioning before return to service.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is treated as ready for service without documenting or communicating the recommissioning requirement.

actions.label: Document requirement
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate that re-commissioning is required before returning the tipper to service.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Commissioning-related HMI context showing that tipper commissioning activities exist elsewhere in the manual.

stop_or_escalate_if: The recommissioning procedure is needed but not available in this source section.
There is any attempt to return the tipper to service without completing the required recommissioning.

source_evidence.text: Record or communicate that re-commissioning is required before returning the tipper to service.
The manual states that a tipper must be re-commissioned after any part is replaced.
Page 194 commissioning section references tipper commissioning activities and controller parameter setup.

success_criteria: It is confirmed whether a tipper part was replaced.
The manual requirement that a tipper must be re-commissioned after any part is replaced is verified.
The recommissioning requirement is recorded or communicated before the tipper is returned to service.
healthy_conditions: Maintenance personnel recognize that any tipper part replacement triggers a recommissioning requirement.
The requirement is documented from the manual and communicated before return to service.
failure_conditions: A tipper part replacement occurs and the recommissioning requirement is not recognized.
The source note cannot be verified from the supplied packet.
The tipper is returned to service without addressing the documented recommissioning requirement.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the maintenance status does not clearly show whether a tipper part was replaced.
Escalate if the actual recommissioning procedure is required, because this source section provides only the requirement note and not the procedure steps.
Escalate before return to service if recommissioning has not been completed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tipper component names and locations for identifying replaced parts.
Nearby maintenance page context in section 7.3.
Nearby maintenance page context in section 7.3.
Commissioning-related HMI context referenced elsewhere in the manual.

missing_details: The supplied source section does not provide the actual recommissioning procedure steps.
The supplied source section does not specify exact documentation method, system, or form for recording the requirement.
The supplied source section does not specify production stop or LOTO requirements for this reference activity.
The supplied source section does not provide a time estimate.
